context: AF Class — développés, grand battement, turns from fifth, rises and demi-pointe work

corrections.text: Don't lift the hip in devant and side développés
corrections.theme: Hip hiking in développés and battement

corrections.text: Don't hike working hip in grand battement to the side
corrections.theme: Hip hiking in développés and battement

corrections.text: Don't overcompensate on the good side — plié only into the max range of the bad ankle
On demi-pointe, toes can actually leave the floor on both feet — identified in private lesson the previous week
Physio noted winging outwards on both feet on demi-pointe, more on the left
Lift arches first before rising onto demi-pointe — was rolling and collapsing arches; chain reaction clicked once found
Pull up on the standing leg — repeated correction, found it increases stability
Turns from fifth: was cheating by not placing foot down with weight before plié — tried variations and found the one that helps stay up
Passé leg stays up even as the turn comes down
